                                                   VOL.        SO NO. 2 AUGUST 1973

                 Joseph M. Finerty, Editor
                 Gloria P. Green, Associate Editor
                 James A. McCall, Associate Editor



                                                                Changes in table formats

                              Beginning with this issue, all tables show data in historical sequence, left to right or top to bottom.
                           For most tables, this means that the earliest data appear in the column closest to the stub, the latest
                           data in the column farthest from the stub. In tables where the data periods are in the stub, the earliest
                           data appear on the top line, the latest data on the bottom line.

                                                                CALENDAR OF FEATURES
                                         In addition to the monthly data appearing regularly in Employment and
                                         Earnings, special features appear in most of the issues, as shown below:

                                                                   Jan.   Feb.   Mar.   Apr.   May   June    July   Aug. Sept.    Oct.   Nov.

                  Revised seasonally adjusted series and
                   current seasonal factors                                x

                   current seasonal factors                                                            (2)

                       The issue that introduces the establishment data adjusted to new benchmarks varies. The October 1972 issue-
                 marks the introduction of March 1971 benchmarks.

           survey week did any work at all as paid employees, in their own        members of the Armed Forces stationed either in the United

                Data for all months since the last benchmark to which the
            series has been adjusted are subject to revision. To provide users
            of the data with a convenient reference source for the revised             Coverage
            data, the BLS publishes as soon as possible after each benchmark
            revision a summary volume of employment, hours, earnings, and                  The BLS sample of establishment employment and payrolls is
            labor turnover statistics, entitled Employment and Earnings,               the largest monthly sampling operation in the field of social
            United States, 1909-72.                                                    statistics. Table G shows the approximate proportion of total

            program is known as "sampling proportionate to average size of
            establishment." This design is an optimum allocation design                Reliability of the employment estimates
